title: Programmatic Markdown-to-HTML Compiler
description: A high-performance markdown parser and layout engine that uses string segmentation instead of regex to convert markdown files into styled HTML.
summary: This module provides a comprehensive suite of tools for parsing markdown syntax, including inline formatting like bold, italics, links, and code spans, as well as block-level elements such as tables, blockquotes, task lists, and footnotes. It features a modular design with both original and extended parsing functions, allowing for flexible compilation of individual files or entire directories. The engine includes built-in styling for generated HTML elements and supports advanced features like Mermaid diagram blocks and footnote collection. It is designed for efficiency, avoiding heavy regex engines in favor of custom string-splitting logic.

code_examples.description: Compile a single markdown file using the full-featured parser.
Batch compile all markdown files within a specific directory.

key_rules: The parser uses custom string segmentation logic rather than standard regular expressions for performance.
Inline formatting like bold and italics must be balanced within the same line to be parsed correctly.
Table parsing requires a header row followed by a separator row of dashes to trigger the table compilation logic.
Footnote definitions must follow the '[^key]: text' format to be correctly extracted by the collector.
The compiler automatically handles block-level state transitions (e.g., closing open lists or tables) when encountering new block types or code markers.
